name: cloud-enum
title: cloud-enum
description:
Multi-cloud open source intelligence tool
cloud_enum enumerates public resources matching user requested keywords in
public clouds:
Amazon Web Services:
Open S3 Buckets
Protected S3 Buckets
Microsoft Azure:
Storage Accounts
Open Blob Storage Containers
Hosted Databases
Virtual Machines
Web Apps
Google Cloud Platform:
Open GCP Buckets
Protected GCP Buckets
Google App Engine sites
.
This program is useful for penetration testing (PENTEST) and network security
analysis serving as OSINT.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use cloud_enum because Multi-cloud open source intelligence tool.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use cloud_enum as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cloud_enum [OPTIONS] [ARGS] ...` as documented upstream for cloud_enum.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cloud_enum -k keyword` as documented upstream for cloud_enum.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cloud_enum -k keyword -t 10` as documented upstream for cloud_enum.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cloud_enum -k somecompany -k somecompany.io -k blockchaindoohickey` as documented upstream for cloud_enum.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair cloud_enum with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `cloud_enum --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
